Former VP Atiku Abubakar felicitates Arsenal on their EPL title win, drawing parallels between their success and his political vision for Nigeria.

Read More: https://punchng.com/atiku-felicitates-arsenal-on-epl-title-triumph/

articles.title: India's muni bond market expands as nearly dozen cities set for debut issues
articles.excerpt:
Investors are also gradually taking greater interest in the
market that ‌was once plagued by concerns over opaque finances
articles.content:
Close to a dozen Indian cities are set to issue municipal bonds for the first time this
year, according to three merchant bankers, as a regulatory and
fiscal push boosts a fledgling market.
Author…

articles.title: A nation of sporting moments, not yet a sporting culture
articles.excerpt: India celebrates sports in flashes, with non-cricket athletes often struggling for recognition and financial security. Despite government schemes, a robust ecosystem for all sports is missing.  Cricket's dominance stems from its established infrastructure and…

articles.title: TN SSLC Result 2026: Water can delivery worker’s daughter M Suganthi scores 496 out of 500, emerges among class 10 toppers
articles.excerpt: M Suganthi, daughter of a water can delivery worker, emerged among Chennai’s top scorers in the TN SSLC Result 2026 by securing 496 out of 500 marks. The Ashok Nagar Girls Higher Secondary School student also ranked second among government school students in …

articles.title: Cave of the Patriarchs: Supreme Court dismisses Waqf petition
articles.excerpt: The judges rejected the petition against the expropriation of space in the cave courtyard. President Yitzhak Amit ruled that the project responds to an essential public need to prevent flooding and to improve freedom of worship.
articles.content: The judges rejected the petition against the expropriation of space in the cave courtyard. President Yitzhak Amit ruled that the project responds to an essential public need to prevent flooding and to improve freedom of worship.
articles.author: Israel National News

articles.title: Citi sees India tightening currency controls to halt Rupee slump
